steps.title: Write the quadratic using its roots
steps.content: Since the graph passes through $(8, 0)$ and $(-5, 0)$, these are the zeros of $f$. The quadratic can be written in factored form: $$f(x) = a(x - 8)(x + 5)$$

steps.title: Expand to find the coefficient b
steps.content: Expanding the factored form: $$f(x) = a(x^2 + 5x - 8x - 40) = a(x^2 - 3x - 40) = ax^2 - 3ax - 40a$$ Comparing with $f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c$, we get $b = -3a$.

steps.title: Apply the constraint to find a + b
steps.content: Now: $$a + b = a + (-3a) = -2a$$ Since $a$ is an integer greater than 1, we have $a \geq 2$, which means $a + b = -2a \leq -4$. When $a = 2$, we get $a + b = -4$. This is the only choice among the options, matching choice D — D is correct.
